content:
type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Finished case_sensitive param for groupby/do_groupby. Couldn't git commit (sandbox has no git author identity configured and I avoid touching git config) so I saved my diff to patch.txt (also backed up at /tmp/agent1_groupby_case_sensitive.patch) and then ran 'git checkout -- src/jinja2/filters.py' to revert the shared working tree back to clean HEAD. The tree is now clean/unmodified - safe for you to edit fresh for with_counts. My change: added case_sensitive=False param, uses ignore_case postprocessor on the sort/group key when False, then remaps each group's key back to the original-case value from the first item via a second make_attrgetter call (output_expr). It only touches the key/expr computation and the final list-comprehension wrapping - if your with_counts change also wraps the final list comprehension, just know mine now returns 'out' (a list) that gets a conditional remap step before the final return, rather than a bare return statement. Ping me if you want to see my exact diff before you start.
